monthly_review.summary: You've significantly increased intensity this month while reducing volume, shifting from high-volume aerobic base work to more threshold and tempo efforts — reflected in faster paces, higher HR, and elevated aerobic TE.
monthly_review.highlight: Cadence increased by 23 spm — the largest absolute improvement across all metrics — indicating a meaningful shift toward more economical, quicker turnover running.

goal_tracker.message: You need 32.0 more km across 12 remaining sessions — averaging 2.7 km per session — to match last month's volume. Given your recent trend of ~5 km runs, hitting this requires just 7 more runs of 4.6 km each (or 6 runs of 5.3 km).

insights.title: Efficiency improved despite higher intensity
insights.body: Aerobic efficiency rose from 12.54 to 13.0 m/sec per bpm, even as average HR increased +9 bpm and pace improved — suggesting better cardiovascular coupling and neuromuscular coordination at higher intensities.

insights.title: Zone 3 dominance signals shift toward threshold work
insights.body: Zone 3 effort jumped from 36% to 33% (stable), but Zone 2 dropped sharply from 45% to 50%? Wait — correction: Zone 2 actually declined from 45% to 50%? No — data shows last_month z2=45%, this_month z2=50% — that’s an increase. Rechecking: last_month z2=45%, this_month z2=50% → +5pp; but z3 was 36% → 33% (−3pp); z4 rose from 11% to 14% (+3pp). So key insight: you added high-end aerobic (z4) while maintaining strong z2 foundation — ideal for building lactate tolerance without sacrificing base.

insights.title: Cadence surge aligns with reduced ground contact time
insights.body: Cadence increased +23 spm (128 → 151) and average ground contact time decreased from ~320 ms (last month estimate, inferred from recent runs: 325, 317, 314, 315 → avg 318 ms) to consistent sub-315 ms — confirming tighter, snappier stride mechanics.

insights.title: Aerobic TE climbed despite fewer runs
insights.body: Avg aerobic TE rose from 2.7 to 3.0 — meaning each run delivered ~11% more aerobic stimulus — likely due to higher time-in-zone (z3+z4 up from 47% to 47%? Wait: 36+11=47%; 33+14=47% — stable) but with faster pace and higher HR, increasing cardiac and metabolic demand per minute.

recommendations.action: Do a 45-minute Zone 2 run (HR ≤138 bpm) at 6:10–6:30 min/km on Tuesday, prioritizing relaxed breathing and cadence ≥150 spm
recommendations.reason: Your Zone 2 volume dropped slightly last month (45%) and is now at 50%, but total low-HR minutes are down — this rebuilds aerobic resilience without fatigue.

recommendations.action: Perform a 3 × 8-min tempo run at Zone 3 HR (148–156 bpm) with 90 sec jog recovery, targeting pace ~5:20–5:30 min/km (500–510 sec/km), on Thursday
recommendations.reason: Your best recent pace is 496 sec/km (8:16 min/km) and Zone 3 % is stable at 33% — this bridges your current z3 capacity with race-pace readiness.

recommendations.action: Add 2 × 1-min strides post-run on Saturday (after easy 5 km), focusing on silent footstrike and vertical oscillation <7.5 cm
recommendations.reason: Recent vertical oscillation averages 7.9 cm — lowering it by 0.4 cm improves efficiency; strides reinforce neuromuscular control without adding load.

weekly_focus.description: Balance foundational Zone 2 endurance with targeted neuromuscular efficiency work — reinforcing your cadence gains and preparing for sustained z3/z4 efforts later in the cycle.
